message:
feat(cli): add conformance matrix command for spec traceability reports

Add 'asupersync conformance matrix' subcommand:

Usage:
  asupersync conformance matrix --root ./src --requirements spec.json

Options:
- --root <DIR>: Root directory to scan (default: current directory)
- --path <PATH>: Additional paths to scan (can be repeated)
- --requirements <FILE>: JSON file with SpecRequirement entries
- --min-coverage <PERCENT>: Minimum coverage to pass (0-100)
- --fail-on-missing: Exit non-zero if any requirements lack coverage

Output (JSON/text/TOON based on --format):
- Total requirements count
- Covered vs missing counts
- Coverage percentage
- Per-requirement test locations
- Scan warnings (parse issues)

Exit codes:
- 0: Success (coverage met or no threshold)
- 1: Coverage below --min-coverage threshold
- 1: Missing requirements when --fail-on-missing specified

Integrates with conformance crate's scan_conformance_attributes() and
TraceabilityMatrix for automated spec compliance verification in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
